records.ety: [OF. eschever, eschiver, eskiver, F. esquiver, fr. OHG. sciuhen, G. scheuen; akin to E. sky. See Shy, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Eshewed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Eshewing.]

defs: To shun; to avoid, as something wrong, or from a feeling of distaste; to keep one's self clear of.
quotes: They must not only eschew evil, but do good.
He who obeys, destruction shall eschew.
